nodes.x: Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,
Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,
Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,
So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went, and did according as the Lord commanded them: the Lord also accepted Job.
And he called Gehazi, and said, Call this Shunammite. So he called her. And when she was come in unto him, he said, Take up thy son.
And he said to Gehazi his servant, Call this Shunammite. And when he had called her, she stood before him.
How long will it be ere ye make an end of words? mark, and afterwards we will speak.
How long will ye vex my soul, and break me in pieces with words?

Oh, Lord, when will the time come when Brother William, Thy
    servant, and myself, shall behold the day that we may stand
    together and gaze upon eternal wisdom engraven upon the heavens,
    while the majesty of our God holdeth up the dark curtain until we
    may read the round of eternity, to the fulness and satisfaction
    of our immortal souls? Oh, Lord, deliver us in due time from…

Oh Lor i, when will the time come; when brother William, tby servant, and myself, shall behold the day that we may stand to* getber and gaze upon eternal wisdom engraven upon the heavens, while the majesty of our God holdeth up the dark curtain, until we may read the round of eternity, to the fulneas and satisfaction of our immortal souls? Oh Lord God ; deliver us in thine own due time from tho…
How long wilt thou speak these things? and how long shall the words of thy mouth be like a strong wind?

and shall bo endoAved with a knowledge of hidden languages; and if he desires and shall seek it at my hands, ho shall bo privileged Avith writing much of my word, as a scribe unto mo for the benefit of my people; therefore this shall he his calling until I shall ‘ order it otlierwiso in my wisdom ; nnd it shall be said of him in time (to come, Behold Wurrcn, the Lord’s scribe for the Lord’s Seer,…
Wherefore are we counted as beasts, and reputed vile in your sight?
Of clean beasts, and of beasts that are not clean, and of fowls, and of every thing that creepeth upon the earth,
He teareth himself in his anger: shall the earth be forsaken for thee? and shall the rock be removed out of his place?

And in fine, wo unto all those who tremble, and are angry because of the truth of God! For behold, he that is built upon the rock receiveth it with gladness; and he that is built upon a sandy foundation trembleth lest he shall fall.
He shall cry unto me, Thou art my father, my God, and the rock of my salvation.
What hast thou here? and whom hast thou here, that thou hast hewed thee out a sepulchre here, as he that heweth him out a sepulchre on high, and that graveth an habitation for himself in a rock?
He only is my rock and my salvation: he is my defence; I shall not be moved.
Yea, the light of the wicked shall be put out, and the spark of his fire shall not shine.
The light of the righteous rejoiceth: but the lamp of the wicked shall be put out.
For there shall be no reward to the evil man; the candle of the wicked shall be put out.
And the wicked shall go away into unquenchable fire, and their end no man knoweth on earth, nor ever shall know, until they come before me in judgment.
And then shall that Wicked be revealed, whom the Lord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth, and shall destroy with the brightness of his coming:
The light shall be dark in his tabernacle, and his candle shall be put out with him.
All darkness shall be hid in his secret places: a fire not blown shall consume him; it shall go ill with him that is left in his tabernacle.
For thou wilt light my candle: the Lord my God will enlighten my darkness.
For thou art my lamp, O Lord: and the Lord will lighten my darkness.

establish his throne in Zion and shall light up the habitations thereof with the glory of his presence, they will not need this light which comes from the bright luminaries that shine forth in yonder heavens, but they will be clothed upon with the glory of their God. When the people meet together in assemblies like this, in their Tabernacles, the Lord will
The steps of his strength shall be straitened, and his own counsel shall cast him down.
His strength shall be hungerbitten, and destruction shall be ready at his side.
Righteousness shall go before him; and shall set us in the way of his steps.
With him is wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding.
For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walketh upon a snare.
The snare is laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way.
He taketh it with his eyes: his nose pierceth through snares.
Lest thou learn his ways, and get a snare to thy soul.
Till a dart strike through his liver; as a bird hasteth to the snare, and knoweth not that it is for his life.
And thus he flattereth them, and leadeth them along until he draggeth their souls down to hell; and thus he causeth them to catch themselves in their own snare.
The gin shall take him by the heel, and the robber shall prevail against him.

And he shall kill the lamb of the trespass offering, and the priest shall take some of the blood of the trespass offering, and put it upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot:
Whose harvest the hungry eateth up, and taketh it even out of the thorns, and the robber swalloweth up their substance.
¶ And he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.
And if he smite out his manservant’s tooth, or his maidservant’s tooth; he shall let him go free for his tooth’s sake.
And he will take the tenth of your seed, and of your vineyards, and give to his officers, and to his servants.
The snare is laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way.
For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walketh upon a snare.
For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.
For among my people are found wicked men: they lay wait, as he that setteth snares; they set a trap, they catch men.
In the transgression of an evil man there is a snare: but the righteous doth sing and rejoice.
Terrors shall make him afraid on every side, and shall drive him to his feet.
His heart is established, he shall not be afraid, until he see his desire upon his enemies.
Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.
Trouble and anguish shall make him afraid; they shall prevail against him, as a king ready to the battle.
Be not a terror unto me: thou art my hope in the day of evil.
He shall not be afraid of evil tidings: his heart is fixed, trusting in the Lord.
His strength shall be hungerbitten, and destruction shall be ready at his side.
The steps of his strength shall be straitened, and his own counsel shall cast him down.
¶ And he shall stand and feed in the strength of the Lord, in the majesty of the name of the Lord his God; and they shall abide: for now shall he be great unto the ends of the earth.
And arms shall stand on his part, and they shall pollute the sanctuary of strength, and shall take away the daily sacrifice, and they shall place the abomination that maketh desolate.
He shall dwell on high: his place of defence shall be the munitions of rocks: bread shall be given him; his waters shall be sure.
¶ He that sacrificeth unto any god, save unto the Lord only, he shall be utterly destroyed.
It shall devour the strength of his skin: even the firstborn of death shall devour his strength.
But he shall acknowledge the son of the hated for the firstborn, by giving him a double portion of all that he hath: for he is the beginning of his strength; the right of the firstborn is his.
And they shall scourge him, and put him to death: and the third day he shall rise again.
But put forth thine hand now, and touch his bone and his flesh, and he will curse thee to thy face.
His confidence shall be rooted out of his tabernacle, and it shall bring him to the king of terrors.
And he shall plant the tabernacles of his palace between the seas in the glorious holy mountain; yet he shall come to his end, and none shall help him.
For the Lord shall be thy confidence, and shall keep thy foot from being taken.
And all his fugitives with all his bands shall fall by the sword, and they that remain shall be scattered toward all winds: and ye shall know that I the Lord have spoken it.
It shall dwell in his tabernacle, because it is none of his: brimstone shall be scattered upon his habitation.
Lord, who shall abide in thy tabernacle? who shall dwell in thy holy hill?
And I will set my tabernacle among you: and my soul shall not abhor you.
And I that am the Lord thy God from the land of Egypt will yet make thee to dwell in tabernacles, as in the days of the solemn feast.
¶ And he put the table in the tent of the congregation, upon the side of the tabernacle northward, without the veil.
His roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off.
He shall not depart out of darkness; the flame shall dry up his branches, and by the breath of his mouth shall he go away.

Say thou, Thus saith the Lord God; Shall it prosper? shall he not pull up the roots thereof, and cut off the fruit thereof, that it wither? it shall wither in all the leaves of her spring, even without great power or many people to pluck it up by the roots thereof.

Nevertheless leave the stump of his roots in the earth, even with a band of iron and brass, in the tender grass of the field; and let it be wet with the dew of heaven, and let his portion be with the beasts in the grass of the earth:
It shall be accomplished before his time, and his branch shall not be green.
For there is hope of a tree, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again, and that the tender branch thereof will not cease.
His remembrance shall perish from the earth, and he shall have no name in the street.
He shall return no more to his house, neither shall his place know him any more.

Now he's gone, we'd not recall him
    From a paradise of bliss,
  Where no evil can befall him,
    To a changing world like this.
  His loved name will never perish,
    Nor his mem'ry crown the dust;
  For the Saints of God will cherish
    The remembrance of the JUST.
His name shall endure for ever: his name shall be continued as long as the sun: and men shall be blessed in him: all nations shall call him blessed.
He shall be driven from light into darkness, and chased out of the world.
He shall fly away as a dream, and shall not be found: yea, he shall be chased away as a vision of the night.
But with an overrunning flood he will make an utter end of the place thereof, and darkness shall pursue his enemies.
And they shall look unto the earth; and behold trouble and darkness, dimness of anguish; and they shall be driven to darkness.
He discovereth deep things out of darkness, and bringeth out to light the shadow of death.
This then is the message which we have heard of him, and declare unto you, that God is light, and in him is no darkness at all.
He shall neither have son nor nephew among his people, nor any remaining in his dwellings.
Let there be none to extend mercy unto him: neither let there be any to favour his fatherless children.
And if he have no daughter, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his brethren.
Neither shall he go in to any dead body, nor defile himself for his father, or for his mother;
They that come after him shall be astonied at his day, as they that went before were affrighted.
These are they whom he shall bring with him, when he shall come in the clouds of heaven to reign on the earth over his people.
And shall deliver him to the Gentiles to mock, and to scourge, and to crucify him: and the third day he shall rise again.
And immediately he rose up before them, and took up that whereon he lay, and departed to his own house, glorifying God.
For whom he did foreknow, he also did predestinate to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brethren.
Surely such are the dwellings of the wicked, and this is the place of him that knoweth not God.

intends to have a house built on the earth. They will probably say—“He has hundreds of them, and has had for many generations. God has houses scattered here and there throughout all the Christian nations, and there never has been a time since the days of the Apostles but what the Lord has had a house, either at Corinth, Athens, or somewhere else; and you can read the inscriptions upon them as you…
For yet a little while, and the wicked shall not be: yea, thou shalt diligently consider his place, and it shall not be.
¶ The curse of the Lord is in the house of the wicked: but he blesseth the habitation of the just.
For ye say, Where is the house of the prince? and where are the dwelling places of the wicked?
